title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Root Cause & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the full lifecycle of FIX-467, a bug where creating the first library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts. The root cause was that the system was using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully fix this issue. This tour follows the investigation in data-flow order: from initial report, through agent triage runs, to the final corrected understanding of the token-selection logic.

steps.description: This is the original ticket filed by the user (FIX-467). The user reported that creating a new library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours. Screenshots were attached as evidence. Key constraint noted here: the agent's available database is not an exact copy of production, but the fix must ensure the new library is created regardless. This ticket context file is the entry point for the entire investigation.
steps.title: Step 1: Original Ticket Report (FIX-467)

steps.description: In this second run, the Helix agent acknowledged the ticket and began triage. The discussion section records the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run focused on understanding the failure mode from attached screenshots and available database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent acknowledgment → environment inspection. The agent is scoping the problem before proposing a fix.
steps.title: Step 2: Agent Acknowledges and Begins Investigation

steps.description: This run (cmp6d2r2h000he30u1tc3mi6b) captures the multi-agent discussion where the user escalated after the initial fix did not resolve the issue. The user clarified the real problem: for NetSuite file cabinet repos, the system was using the user's personal token rather than the built-in Helix system token for repository creation. The Helix agent confirmed it understood this as the actual root cause. This discussion is critical — it reveals the surface fix from the prior run addressed a symptom, not the underlying token-selection logic.
steps.title: Step 3: Root Cause Identified — Wrong Token Used for Repo Creation

steps.description: The user's follow-up message at line 29 explicitly states: 'It shouldn't be using the user's token. It should be using the built-in Helix token that creates repositories.' This is the pivotal statement that redirected the fix. The agent's response confirms it grasped the distinction between user-scoped credentials and system-level Helix credentials, and that the token-selection path needed to be corrected specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repository creation flows.
steps.title: Step 4: User Clarifies Token Requirement — System Token vs User Token

steps.description: This is the final run (cmp7kjf350029ks0uhdwmf39s) where the corrected fix was applied and verified. The ticket.md for this run aggregates the full discussion history, including the multi-turn escalation and the agent's revised understanding. This file serves as the authoritative record of the complete fix lifecycle: initial report → surface fix → escalation → root cause diagnosis → final correct fix. Reviewing this file confirms the entire resolution chain is documented.
steps.title: Step 5: Final Run — Corrected Fix Applied and Verified

steps.description: This CodeTour JSON artifact was generated as part of the final run to document the walkthrough for future developers. It records each investigation step, the root cause (incorrect token selection for NetSuite file cabinet repos), and the resolution path. The tour confirms that the major code paths remain clean: repository creation now routes through the Helix system token rather than the user's personal token, preventing this class of failure for any user creating their first library ticket in any environment.
steps.title: Step 6: Summary — CodeTour Artifact and Code Path Verification
